hero.headline: Dr. MGR Online MBA in Operations Management: Fees, Syllabus and Career Scope
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved 2-year programme | NAAC A+ accredited | Rs 1,36,000 - Rs 1,41,000 total fee | Deemed University since 2003

sections.tldr: Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute Online MBA in Operations Management covers Purchase and Inventory Management, Lean and Six Sigma Management, and Total Quality Management in Sem 3. Common Sem 4 core includes Digital Marketing, International Business Management, and an 8-week mandatory Field Work and Project Work. Total fee Rs 1,36,000 to Rs 1,41,000 across 4 semesters. NAAC A+ accredited, UGC-DEB approved, Deemed University.

about.body:
Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute is a Deemed University in Chennai, Tamil Nadu, named after former Chief Minister Dr. M.G. Ramachandran. The institution has operated under UGC recognition since 2003 and holds NAAC A+ accreditation with UGC-DEB approval for online programmes.

The Operations Management specialisation concentrates three dedicated subjects in Sem 3: Purchase and Inventory Management, Lean and Six Sigma Management, and Total Quality Management. This is the strongest operations subject count among the six Dr. MGR MBA specialisations, tied with Finance Management. Sem 4 shares common core subjects.

Chennai is a major manufacturing hub (automotive, electronics, heavy engineering). The operations management curriculum aligns with the local industrial ecosystem. Hyundai, Royal Enfield, Ashok Leyland, Caterpillar, and multiple auto component manufacturers operate in the Chennai corridor. The 8-week mandatory field project can be completed in a manufacturing plant, logistics centre, or quality department.

At Rs 1,36,000 to Rs 1,41,000 total, the programme sits in the mid-range for NAAC A+ online MBAs.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Operations Management specialisation subjects. Spec-specific subjects listed first, followed by common core.

subjects.name: Purchase and Inventory Management
subjects.desc: Procurement strategies, vendor evaluation, EOQ models, JIT inventory, ABC analysis, safety stock calculations, and warehouse management.

subjects.name: Lean and Six Sigma Management
subjects.desc: Lean principles, 5S implementation, value stream mapping, DMAIC methodology, Six Sigma tools (fishbone, Pareto, control charts), and waste elimination.

subjects.name: Total Quality Management
subjects.desc: TQM philosophy, Deming's 14 points, Kaizen, quality circles, ISO 9001 implementation, and quality cost analysis.

subjects.name: Strategic Management
subjects.desc: Competitive strategy frameworks, industry analysis, manufacturing strategy alignment, and strategic implementation.

subjects.name: Entrepreneurship Development
subjects.desc: Manufacturing startup ideation, business plan creation, funding for SME manufacturing, and MSME support systems.

subjects.name: Management Accounting
subjects.desc: Cost accounting for manufacturing, budgetary control, standard costing, variance analysis, and make-or-buy decisions.

subjects.name: Production and Operations Management
subjects.desc: Production planning and control, facility layout, capacity planning, scheduling, and operations strategy frameworks.

subjects.name: Skill Development
subjects.desc: Communication skills, shop floor presentation techniques, team management, and professional development.

subjects.name: Summer Project
subjects.desc: Industry-linked summer project in a manufacturing or operations setup requiring process analysis, data collection, and structured report.

subjects.name: Digital Marketing
subjects.desc: Digital marketing fundamentals, B2B marketing for manufacturing, industrial marketing strategies, and digital analytics.

subjects.name: International Business Management
subjects.desc: Global supply chain management, export/import operations, international manufacturing strategy, and cross-border logistics.

subjects.name: Contemporary Seminar
subjects.desc: Student-led seminar on current operations management topics. Research, presentation, and peer discussion on Industry 4.0 trends.

subjects.name: Field Work and Project Work (8 Weeks)
subjects.desc: Mandatory 8-week field project in a manufacturing plant, logistics centre, or quality department. Process improvement study, structured report, and viva voce defence.

whoHires.body:
Dr. MGR MBA Operations Management graduates target roles across manufacturing, supply chain, quality, and logistics:

**Industries:** Automotive manufacturing, auto components, electronics manufacturing, pharmaceutical production, FMCG manufacturing, logistics and warehousing, heavy engineering, and construction

**Entry-level roles:** Production Executive, Quality Analyst, Supply Chain Coordinator, Inventory Controller, Purchase Officer, Lean Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years):** Operations Manager, Quality Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Production Manager, Lean Six Sigma Black Belt, Warehouse Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Operations, Plant Head, Director of Manufacturing, Chief Operating Officer, Head of Supply Chain, Director of Quality

**Employers:** Hyundai (Sriperumbudur), Royal Enfield, Ashok Leyland, TVS Motor, Caterpillar, Saint-Gobain, Foxconn, auto component clusters (Ambattur, Oragadam), and Chennai port logistics companies.

**Note:** Chennai's Sriperumbudur-Oragadam automotive corridor is India's largest auto manufacturing cluster. Dr. MGR's local brand provides direct access to this ecosystem.

skills.body:
The Operations Management specialisation builds skills across procurement, lean manufacturing, and quality management:

**Technical skills:**
- Purchase and Inventory: vendor evaluation matrices, EOQ calculation, safety stock optimization, ABC/VED analysis, JIT implementation, and warehouse layout design
- Lean Six Sigma: value stream mapping, 5S audit execution, DMAIC project leadership, statistical process control (SPC), and root cause analysis using Six Sigma tools
- Total Quality Management: ISO 9001 internal audit, quality circle facilitation, Kaizen event management, Deming cycle application, and cost of quality calculation

**Applied skills:**
- Production Planning: MRP/MRP-II concepts, production scheduling, capacity requirement planning, and shop floor control
- Process Improvement: bottleneck identification, cycle time reduction, OEE (Overall Equipment Effectiveness) measurement, and continuous improvement project management

**Soft skills:** Cross-functional team leadership on the shop floor, supplier negotiation, presenting process improvement data to plant leadership, and managing change during lean transformation.

comparisons.body:
Comparing Dr. MGR Online MBA Operations Management against peer programmes:

**vs Vignan Online MBA LSCM (Rs 90,000):** Vignan is Rs 46,000 to Rs 51,000 cheaper with a Logistics and Supply Chain Management focus. Dr. MGR covers Purchase/Inventory, Lean Six Sigma, and TQM (manufacturing-heavy). Vignan's LSCM is logistics-focused. Dr. MGR is manufacturing/quality-focused. Choose based on whether your career is in logistics or manufacturing operations.

**vs Alliance Online MBA Operations (Rs 1,75,000):** Alliance is Rs 34,000 to Rs 39,000 more expensive. Alliance has a stronger national brand for management education. Dr. MGR has NAAC A+ at a lower fee and three dedicated operations subjects in Sem 3. Alliance wins on brand. Dr. MGR wins on fee and operations depth.

**vs Kurukshetra University Online MBA (Rs 1,02,000):** Kurukshetra does not offer a dedicated operations management specialisation. If operations/manufacturing management is your goal, Dr. MGR's dedicated Purchase/Inventory, Lean Six Sigma, and TQM subjects provide structured coverage that a general MBA cannot match.

fees.body:
The Dr. MGR Online MBA in Operations Management is priced between Rs 1,36,000 and Rs 1,41,000 total across 4 semesters (2-year programme). Fee range varies slightly by specialization - contact our counsellor for the exact figure for your chosen track.

**Payment breakdown**:
- **Per semester**: Rs 34,000 - Rs 40,000 across 4 semesters
- **Full upfront option**: Rs 1,36,000 - Rs 1,41,000 one-time

**Additional charges**:
- Registration fee: Rs 1,000 (one-time, non-refundable at admission)

**Zero-cost EMI**: Available directly through Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute starting Rs 5,700 per month. No external marketing partner or NBFC intermediary.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees with Dr. MGR Online each quarter. Please reconfirm current fees for Operations Management with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Operations Management as the specialisation, issued by Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute (Deemed University) under UGC-DEB online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A+ accreditation, UGC-DEB approval, and Deemed University status since 2003. Valid for operations manager roles, manufacturing management positions, quality manager certifications (ISO lead auditor eligibility), and private-sector plant management careers. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three reviews from Dr. MGR Online MBA Operations Management students. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Sathish
items.liked: Lean and Six Sigma Management as a standalone subject is directly applicable to my auto component factory. Chennai's manufacturing corridor employers recognize Dr. MGR immediately
items.disliked: Sem 4 has no operations-specific subjects. All the depth is front-loaded in Sem 3
items.body: I work at an auto component plant in Oragadam. Dr. MGR is the obvious choice for Chennai manufacturing professionals. Three Sem 3 operations subjects (purchase/inventory, lean/six sigma, TQM) cover exactly what plant managers need. The 8-week field project in my own factory let me run a real DMAIC improvement cycle. For Chennai automotive and manufacturing careers, the brand works perfectly.

items.name: Ramya
items.liked: Total Quality Management covered ISO 9001 and quality circles at a practical level useful for my textile manufacturing quality role
items.disliked: Pan-India brand recognition is limited. Manufacturing companies in Pune or Gujarat may not know Dr. MGR
items.body: The operations curriculum is compact but covers the right areas: procurement, lean/six sigma, and TQM. Three dedicated subjects in one semester. The common core adds Production and Operations Management in the same semester, so Sem 3 is heavily operations-loaded. For Tamil Nadu manufacturing careers (auto, textiles, electronics), Dr. MGR works. For national manufacturing brands, the brand gap is a consideration.

items.name: Manoj
items.liked: Purchase and Inventory Management covered EOQ, ABC analysis, and JIT at enough depth to apply in my warehouse operations role
items.disliked: LMS interface needs modernization. Some study materials reference older manufacturing standards. Faculty responsiveness is inconsistent
items.body: At Rs 1.36-1.41L, Vignan LSCM at Rs 90,000 is cheaper but focuses on logistics, not manufacturing operations. Alliance Operations at Rs 1,75,000 costs more but has national brand. Dr. MGR is the mid-range option with strong Chennai manufacturing access. If you are in the Chennai auto corridor, this is the most locally relevant operations MBA. For Pune, NCR, or Gujarat manufacturing hubs, the brand does not travel well.

faqs.question: Is Dr. MGR a Deemed University?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute has operated as a Deemed University under UGC recognition since 2003. Named after former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister Dr. M.G. Ramachandran.

faqs.question: What is the fee difference between Rs 1.36L and Rs 1.41L?
faqs.answer: The total fee varies slightly by specialization. Contact our counsellor to confirm the exact fee for Operations Management before making any payment.

faqs.question: Is Dr. MGR Online MBA UGC-DEB approved?
faqs.answer: Yes. Dr. MGR Educational and Research Institute is UGC-DEB approved for online programmes. The degree is legally equivalent to a campus MBA under UGC 2020 guidelines.

faqs.question: Does the Operations MBA cover Lean Six Sigma?
faqs.answer: Yes. Lean and Six Sigma Management is a standalone Semester 3 subject covering lean principles, 5S, value stream mapping, DMAIC methodology, and Six Sigma statistical tools. This is a full-semester course, not a module.

faqs.question: Is this MBA relevant for Chennai manufacturing professionals?
faqs.answer: Yes. Chennai's Sriperumbudur-Oragadam corridor is India's largest auto manufacturing cluster. Dr. MGR's local brand, NAAC A+ grade, and operations-specific curriculum (procurement, lean/six sigma, TQM) align directly with manufacturing management career needs.

chooseIf: You work in Chennai's manufacturing corridor (automotive, electronics, engineering) and want a locally recognized operations MBA.
Lean Six Sigma, TQM, and Purchase/Inventory Management as dedicated subjects match your career direction.
NAAC A+ at a mid-range fee (Rs 1.36-1.41L) meets your requirements.
The 8-week mandatory field project in a manufacturing plant or quality department is valuable for process improvement experience.
consider: Vignan LSCM at Rs 90,000 is cheaper but focuses on logistics, not manufacturing operations.
Alliance Operations at Rs 1,75,000 has stronger national brand if budget allows.
Sem 4 has no operations-specific subjects. All spec depth is in Sem 3.
For Pune, NCR, or Gujarat manufacturing careers, Dr. MGR's regional brand may not carry sufficient weight.
